title: Energized Smoky Vietnamese Peel Cha Gio
description: Crispy, herb‑infused spring rolls filled with smoked tofu and mixed vegetable peels, finished with a smoky glaze for a monsoon‑inspired kick.
instructions: Cook rice vermicelli in boiling water until al dente, then drain and set aside.

In a bowl, combine ground chicken, diced smoked tofu, cooked vermicelli, shredded carrot peel, cucumber peel, bean sprouts, scallions, soy sauce, fish sauce, ground pepper, and smoked paprika; mix until well incorporated.
Soak each rice paper wrapper in warm water for 30 seconds to soften, then lay flat on a clean surface.
Spoon about 2 tablespoons of the filling onto the center of each wrapper and fold the sides inward, rolling tightly to form a cylinder.
Preheat a large skillet or cazuela over medium‑high heat and add a splash of oil, then place the rolled cha gio seam side down.
Cook for 3‑4 minutes, turning occasionally, until the wrappers are golden brown and crispy.
Remove from heat, squeeze fresh lime juice over the cha gio, and serve immediately.
For a smoky finish, finish each roll in a sheet of parchment over a gentle smoke source or add a few drops of smoked oil just before serving.
